sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Rocket Lab은 2026년 1분기 매출 2억 34만 달러를 기록하며 전년 대비 63%의 높은 성장세를 보였습니다.

keyPoints.text: 우주 시스템 부문 매출이 4,970만 달러 증가하며 성장을 견인했고, 발사 서비스 매출도 2,810만 달러 늘었습니다.
발사 서비스 성장은 Electron 발사 횟수 증가(6회)와 HASTE 서브오비탈 테스트 미션의 매출 인식에 기인합니다.

riskChanges.text: 2026년 2월 26일 제출된 연례 보고서(Form 10-K)에 공시된 위험 요인 외에 중대한 변경 사항은 없습니다.

businessChanges.text: 중형 발사체 Neutron 개발 및 우주선 부품 포트폴리오 확장을 위해 연구개발(R&D)에 지속적인 투자를 진행 중입니다.
공개 기업으로서의 운영 비용 및 인프라 투자 확대로 인해 판매비와 관리비는 절대 금액 기준으로 증가할 전망입니다.

financialHighlights.text: 분기 매출 2억 34만 달러를 달성했으나, 영업이익은 -5,596만 달러, 순이익은 -4,502만 달러로 적자가 지속되었습니다.
총자산 28억 1,994만 달러를 보유하고 있으며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 12억 549만 달러로 안정적인 유동성을 확보했습니다.

watchItems.text: 매출 성장세는 뚜렷하나 영업 적자가 지속되고 있어, 향후 Neutron 등 신규 프로젝트의 상업화 속도와 비용 효율화 달성 여부가 수익성 개선의 핵심입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2026-03-31 revenue = 2억 34만 달러 (USD 0.2B; 200 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31. Readable Korean unit: 2억 달러.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 operating income = -5,596만 달러 (USD -0.056B; -56 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31. Readable Korean unit: 5,600만 달러.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 net income = -4,502만 달러 (USD -0.045B; -45 million), XBRL range 2026-01-01..2026-03-31. Readable Korean unit: 4,500만 달러.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 total assets = 28억 1,994만 달러 (USD 2.82B; 2,820 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31. Readable Korean unit: 28억 2,000만 달러.
Quarter ended 2026-03-31 cash and cash equivalents = 12억 549만 달러 (USD 1.205B; 1,205 million), XBRL range instant..2026-03-31. Readable Korean unit: 12억 500만 달러.

Item 1A. Risk Factors There have been no material changes from the risk factors previously disclosed in Part I, Item 1A of our Annual Report on Form 10-K, as filed with the SEC on February 26, 2026. <div

Revenue Growth Three Months Ended March 31, 2026 and 2025 We generated $200.3 million and $122.6 million in revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025, respectively, representing a year-on-year increase in revenue of approximately 63%. This year-on-year increase resulted from space systems revenue growth of $49.7 million, primarily driven by satellite manufacturing, and an increase in launch revenue of $28.1 million. Launch revenue growth was due to a higher launch cadence with six Electron launch missions completed for the three months ended March 31, 2026, versus five launch missions completed for the three months ended March 31, 2025, revenue recognized on over-time Hypersonic Accelerator Suborbital Test Electron (“HASTE”) launch missions, an increase in other   Normalized numeric aliases: $200.3 million = 2억 30만 달러; $122.6 million = 1억 2,260만 달러; $49.7 million = 4,970만 달러; $28.1 million = 2,810만 달러; $200.3 = 200.3달러; $122.6 = 122.6달러; $49.7 = 49.7달러; $28.1 = 28.1달러.

Research and Development, Net Research and development expenses consist primarily of labor, prototype, professional services, materials, facilities and depreciation expense. We intend to continue to make significant investments in developing new products and enhancing existing products, including but not limited to our medium capacity Neutron launch vehicle and spacecraft features and capabilities, as well as expanding our portfolio of spacecraft components and subsystems. Research and development expenses will be variable relative to the number of products that are in development, validation or testing. However, we expect it to decline as a percentage of total revenue over time. 32 Table of Contents Selling,

General and Administrative Selling, general and administrative expenses consist primarily of personnel-related expenses for our sales, marketing, supply chain, finance, legal, human resources and administrative personnel, as well as the costs of customer service, information technology, risk management and related insurance, travel, allocated overhead, other marketing, communications, administrative and transaction expenses. We also expect to further invest in our corporate infrastructure and incur additional expenses associated with operating as a public company, including increased legal and accounting costs, investor relations and compliance costs. As a result, we expect that selling, general and administrative expenses will increase in absolute dollars in future periods but decline as a percentage of total revenue over time.
